summary: Close the regular-CI M2 findings by executing desktop-shell Rust tests, running browser acceptance on Linux and Windows, and removing obsolete Q-0001 blocker copy without changing the pinned engine.
specSections: Section 2.7 test-first evidence and milestone gates

decisions.decision: Keep the established Ubuntu web and native verification, add the missing desktop-shell cargo test to the native job, and move browser acceptance into a fail-independent ubuntu-latest/windows-latest matrix with operating-system-appropriate pinned Chromium installation. Retain every established check and the exact OpenSCAD 2026.06.12 URL, checksum, and version assertion.
decisions.rationale: V-2 requires the product's unit and integration tests on every pull request, including the desktop shell, while V-4 explicitly requires end-to-end execution on Windows and Linux. A dedicated two-platform acceptance matrix supplies real operating-system lanes without duplicating the complete web verification suite or weakening the native engine gate.
specBasis: Section 2.7 test-first evidence and milestone gates

testEvidence.criterion: The regular CI workflow runs desktop-shell tests, contains no resolved-Q-0001 blocker label, and defines meaningful Linux and Windows browser-acceptance lanes
testEvidence.test: Read only .github/workflows/ci.yml and require the exact desktop cargo-test command, absence of Q-0001, an ubuntu-latest/windows-latest matrix, both operating-system browser-install branches, and the Playwright acceptance command.
